metadata.title: Money field
metadata.description: The money field component collects monetary values from users with built-in currency formatting and validation. Use money field for prices, costs, or financial amounts to provide proper currency symbols, decimal handling, and numeric validation.

properties.description: The highest decimal or integer value accepted for the field. When used with step, the value rounds down to the maximum number. Users can still type values higher than the maximum using the keyboard. Implement validation to enforce this constraint.

The lowest decimal or integer value accepted for the field. When used with step, the value rounds up to the minimum number. Users can still type values lower than the minimum using the keyboard. Implement validation to enforce this constraint.

The current monetary value in the field as a string. When setting this property programmatically, it updates the field's display value. When reading it, you get the user's current input. The value should be a numeric string representing the amount in the store's currency.

Controls browser autofill behavior for the field. Basic values: on - Enables autofill without specifying content type (default)off - Disables autofill for sensitive data or one-time codes Specific field values describe the expected data type. You can optionally prefix these with: section-${string} - Scopes autofill to a specific form section (when multiple forms exist on the same page)shipping or billing - Indicates whether the data is for shipping or billing purposesBoth section and group (for example, section-primary shipping email) Providing a specific autofill token helps browsers suggest more relevant saved data. Learn more about the set of autocomplete values supported in browsers.

The initial value of the field when it first loads. Unlike placeholder, this is a real value that the user can edit and that gets submitted with the form. Once the user starts typing, their input replaces it. Changing this property after the field has loaded has no effect. To update the field value at any time, use value instead.

Supplementary text displayed below the checkbox to provide additional context, instructions, or help. Use this to explain what checking the box means or provide guidance to users. This text is announced to screen readers.
An error message displayed below the checkbox to indicate validation problems. When set, the checkbox is styled with error indicators and the message is announced to screen readers.

The text displayed as the field label, which identifies the purpose of the field to users. This label is associated with the field for accessibility and helps users understand what information to provide.

Controls whether the label is visible to all users or only to screen readers. visible: The label is shown to everyone (default).exclusive: The label is visually hidden but still announced by screen readers. Use exclusive when the surrounding context makes the label redundant visually, but screen reader users still need it for clarity.
The placeholder text displayed in the field when it's empty, providing a hint about the expected input format or value.
Whether the field is read-only and can't be edited. Read-only fields remain focusable and their content is announced by screen readers.

Whether the field requires a value before form submission. Displays a visual indicator and adds semantic meaning, but doesn't automatically validate or show errors. Use the error property to display validation messages.
Whether the field is disabled, preventing any user interaction.
A unique identifier for the element. Use this to reference the element in JavaScript, link labels to form controls, or target specific elements for styling or scripting.
The name attribute for the field, used to identify the field's value when the form is submitted. Must be unique within the nearest containing form.

examples.name: Main example
examples.html_code:
<s-money-field
  label="Regional Price"
  placeholder="99.99"
  details="Recommended price for the European market"
></s-money-field>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.money_field :regional_price, label: "Regional Price", placeholder: "99.99", details: "Recommended price for the European market" %>

examples.name: Collect a currency value
examples.description: Capture monetary values with automatic currency formatting. This example pairs a label with placeholder text and helper details.
examples.html_code:
<s-money-field
  label="Regional Price"
  placeholder="99.99"
  details="Recommended price for the European market"
></s-money-field>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.money_field :regional_price, label: "Regional Price", placeholder: "99.99", details: "Recommended price for the European market" %>

examples.name: Add a label and constraints
examples.description: Set input boundaries for valid amounts. This example configures min/max limits to constrain the accepted value range.

examples.name: Handle validation errors
examples.description: Communicate input problems clearly to users. This example displays an error message when the entered value is invalid.
examples.html_code:
<s-money-field
  label="Product cost"
  min="0.01"
  error="Product cost is required"
></s-money-field>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.money_field :product_cost, label: "Product cost", min: "0.01", error: "Product cost is required" %>

examples.name: Combine multiple fields in a form
examples.description: Collect multiple monetary values in a single form. This example groups money fields for price, compare-at price, and cost with individual constraints.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack direction="block" gap="base">
  <s-money-field
    label="Price"
    value="0.00"
    min="0.01"
    max="99999.99"
    details="Customers will see this price"
  ></s-money-field>


  <s-money-field
    label="Compare at price"
    value=""
    min="0"
    max="99999.99"
    details="Show customers the original price when on sale"
  ></s-money-field>


  <s-money-field
    label="Cost per item"
    value=""
    min="0"
    max="99999.99"
    details="Customers won't see this"
  ></s-money-field>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack direction="block" gap="base">
  <%= form.money_field :price, label: "Price", value: "0.00", min: "0.01", max: "99999.99", details: "Customers will see this price" %>


  <%= form.money_field :compare_at_price, label: "Compare at price", value: true, min: "0", max: "99999.99", details: "Show customers the original price when on sale" %>


  <%= form.money_field :cost_per_item, label: "Cost per item", value: true, min: "0", max: "99999.99", details: "Customers won't see this" %>
</s-stack>
